intro: Soldanella stiriaca, also known as Alpine snowbell, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Primulaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties that may help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Soldanella stiriaca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
